29.reason: [Error: variable declaration after executable statement is not allowed in C89; causes compilation failure if compiler strictly adheres to C89 standard]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Compilation error: declarations must come before executable statements in C89; this line will cause a compilation failure.]

84.reason: [Error: When `list` points to the last real node, `list->link` is NULL, causing `p` to be NULL. The subsequent `printf` dereferences `p`, leading to undefined behavior (segmentation fault).]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When `list` points to the last real node, `list->link` is NULL, causing `p` to be NULL. The subsequent `printf` dereferences `p`, leading to undefined behavior (segmentation fault).]

83.reason: [Logical error: the loop should iterate over real nodes, not the dummy head. This condition causes p to become NULL after the last node, leading to a segmentation fault when printf tries to dereference p.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Logical error: the loop should iterate over real nodes, not the dummy head. This condition causes p to become NULL after the last node, leading to a segmentation fault when printf tries to dereference p.]

85.reason: Null pointer dereference when p is NULL (at the end of the list)
